name: Drone CI
what_it_does: Container-native CI/CD system with a REST API for pipeline management, build triggering, and status retrieval.
use_cases: Triggering and monitoring CI/CD pipelines programmatically via REST API
Automating build status checks in deployment workflows
Managing pipeline secrets and environment variables through API
Integrating CI/CD status into custom dashboards or alerting systems
Orchestrating multi-repo build workflows with an agent coordinating across pipelines
not_for: Teams requiring a hosted SaaS CI solution with zero infrastructure management
Non-containerized build environments or Windows-native build pipelines
best_when: Best when you need a lightweight, container-native CI/CD system that you self-host and want to control programmatically via a clean REST API.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need a managed cloud CI service with no infrastructure overhead, or need deep GitHub Actions ecosystem compatibility.

auth.notes: Bearer token authentication. Tokens generated via OAuth login flow or admin-created personal access tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free when self-hosted; unlimited builds
pricing.paid_tiers: Harness Cloud CI from $25/user/month for managed hosting
pricing.notes: Open-source version (now part of Harness Gitness) is fully free self-hosted. Cloud plans through Harness.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no rate limits. Harness Cloud: plan-dependent

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns standard HTTP status codes with JSON error bodies; documentation has gaps in edge cases.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Build triggers are not idempotent — each API call creates a new build run.
known_agent_gotchas: Build trigger API always creates a new build even if one is already running for the same commit
API docs are split between old drone.io docs and new Harness docs, causing confusion
Self-hosted instances require custom base URL configuration

security_score_components.security_notes: Secret storage for pipeline credentials is built-in. TLS depends on self-hosted deployment configuration.
